fundamentals.name: Netflix, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Netflix, Inc. provides entertainment services worldwide. The company offers television (TV) series, documentaries, feature films, games, and live programming across various genres and languages. It also provides members the ability to receive streaming content through a host of internet-connected devices, including TVs, digital video players, TV set-top boxes, and mobile devices. Netflix, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Los Gatos, California.

judgment.summary: Netflix exhibits strong revenue and earnings growth, high margins, and robust free cash flow, trading at a reasonable forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 1.4%; next earnings date is 2026-07-16.
positives: Revenue growth 16.2% YoY and earnings growth 86.4% YoY indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Operating margin 32.3% and profit margin 28.5% demonstrate significant profitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $25.99B is exceptionally strong, supporting business operations and investments.
Return on equity at 48.5% signifies efficient use of shareholder capital.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $114.56, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: The PEG ratio of 1.74 suggests growth expectations may be partially priced in.
Debt/Equity ratio of 53.79 indicates a moderate level of leverage.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 1.4%, a minor negative signal.
Beta of 1.55 implies higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Netflix's competitive position in the streaming landscape was recently questioned, though the article did not provide specific details to support or refute this. The company's last reported quarter showed a slight EPS miss, with the next earnings date set for mid-July. Traders should monitor commentary around subscriber growth and content spending ahead of the upcoming earnings release.
